senses.definition: to a point where one is very tired and has no more energy to keep on

examples.x: They keep running until they drop, training for the upcoming marathon.
She studies tirelessly till she drops, determined to ace the exam.
The team works non-stop until they drop, striving to complete the project on time.
He ran till he dropped, giving his all in the final minutes of the game.
We worked late into the night till we dropped, trying to meet the deadline.
She danced until she dropped, showcasing her passion on the stage.

word_list.pronunciation: fɹʌm ðə kɹˈeɪdəl tʊ ðə ɡɹˈeɪv

senses.definition: through the whole life span of a person

examples.x: We must strive to provide quality education from cradle to grave.
He cherishes the love and support he has received from cradle to the grave.
They advocate for equal opportunities for all individuals from the cradle to grave.
She experienced a fulfilling life journey from the cradle to the grave.
The society had a strong sense of community and support from cradle to the grave.
They ensured that healthcare services were accessible from the cradle to grave.
We will continue to prioritize holistic development from the cradle to the grave.
I've known him for donkey's years, we go way back.
They have been waiting for their turn in line for donkey's years.
We worked together for donkey's years before he retired.
They had been friends for donkey's years until they drifted apart.
She had been studying that subject for donkey's years before finally mastering it.
They will be traveling for donkey's years, exploring different countries.
I will have known her for donkey's years by the time we reach that milestone.

senses.definition: to have a long history or past relationship with someone or something

examples.x: The company and its suppliers go back a long way, with a history of collaboration.
The two nations went back a long way, with a history of diplomatic relations.
John and Mary go back a long way.
My car and I go back a long way; it's been a reliable companion for over a decade.
This book and I go back a long way; I've had it since my college days, and it's traveled with me through different phases of life.
My colleague and I go back a long way, having worked together on various projects over the years.
Our families go back a long way, and we've celebrated many holidays together.
Tom and I go back a long way; we've been friends since kindergarten.

senses.definition: in a way that is very regular or precisely as planned

examples.x: The train arrives at the station like clockwork every morning at 8:00 a.m.
She wakes up at 6:00 a.m. every day like clockwork, ready to start her morning routine.
The factory's production line operates like clockwork, ensuring consistent output and efficiency.
The project ran smoothly, with each phase completing like clockwork according to the schedule.
The team executed their plan flawlessly, and everything fell into place like clockwork.
The event went off without a hitch; the logistics were managed like clockwork.
The event organizers have meticulously planned everything, ensuring that the schedule will run like clockwork.

senses.definition: non-stop and continuing through the whole day and night

examples.x: The customer service hotline operates around the clock to assist customers at any time.
They work around the clock to meet the project deadline.
The security team patrols the premises around the clock to ensure safety.
The team worked around the clock to complete the construction project ahead of schedule.
The emergency response team operated around the clock during the natural disaster.

senses.definition: something that cannot continue to work or make progress without stops or interruptions

examples.x: The project is progressing in fits and starts, with periods of intense activity followed by long periods of stagnation.
He works in fits and starts, sometimes being extremely productive and other times struggling to make progress.
Their relationship is characterized by fits and starts, with moments of intense passion followed by periods of silence and distance.
The company's growth happened in fits and starts, with periods of rapid expansion followed by financial setbacks.
She studied for the exam in fits and starts, alternating between intense preparation and periods of procrastination.
The construction project experienced delays in fits and starts, with unexpected issues causing intermittent pauses in the construction progress.
The project is likely to proceed in fits and starts, considering the unpredictability of external factors and resource availability.

senses.definition: happening or done on a daily basis
